slides.title: Grids and Matrices 🧮
slides.content:
Sometimes your data is an array of arrays. 

For example, a Tic-Tac-Toe board is just a grid: an array containing 3 arrays, where each inner array holds 3 strings ("X", "O", or "").

slides.title: Typing 2D Arrays
slides.content: To type this, you simply stack the bracket pairs together: `type[][]`.

quizData.question: How would you type a 3D array (an array of arrays of arrays) holding numbers?
quizData.explanation: You just add another pair of brackets! `number[][][]`.

slides.title: Empty Initializations ⚠️
slides.content: If you create an empty 2D array without a type annotation, TS might not know what goes inside later.

slides.title: Be Explicit
slides.content: When doing this, explicit annotation is required:
